questions.q: Shalya was the king of which realm?
questions.explanation: Shalya ruled the northwestern kingdom of Madra, homeland of his sister Madri.

questions.q: Who was Shalya's sister?
questions.explanation: Shalya's sister was Madri, the second wife of King Pandu and mother of the twins.

questions.q: Which Pandavas were Shalya's nephews?
questions.explanation: As Madri's brother, Shalya was the maternal uncle of her sons Nakula and Sahadeva.

questions.q: How was Shalya drawn into fighting for Duryodhana?
options: He lost a wager to Shakuni
He accepted lavish hospitality before knowing whose it was
questions.explanation: Duryodhana secretly built splendid rest camps along Shalya's march, and the pleased king promised a boon before learning his host's identity.

questions.q: Whose chariot did Shalya drive during the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: On the seventeenth day Shalya served as Karna's charioteer in his great duel against Arjuna.

questions.q: To whom did Shalya give a secret promise before the war?
questions.explanation: After confessing he was bound to the Kauravas, Shalya secretly promised Yudhishthira he would undermine Karna's confidence.

questions.q: What did Shalya secretly do while driving Karna's chariot?
options: He passed plans to the Pandavas
He disparaged Karna and praised his foe
He blunted Karna's arrows at night
questions.explanation: Keeping his promise to Yudhishthira, Shalya belittled Karna from the chariot box to sap his fighting spirit.

questions.q: What was Shalya's final position in the Kaurava army?
options: Leader of the Madra cavalry alone
questions.explanation: After Karna's death Shalya was made the fourth and final commander-in-chief of the Kaurava forces.

questions.q: Who killed Shalya at Kurukshetra?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira, the mildest of the Pandavas, slew Shalya in their duel on the war's final day.

questions.q: On which day of the war did Shalya fall?
questions.explanation: Shalya led the Kaurava army on the eighteenth morning and was killed by midday of that final day.

questions.q: Whom was Shalya originally marching to join when Duryodhana's hospitality waylaid him?
questions.explanation: Shalya had set out with his army to fight for his nephews and the Pandavas, assuming the splendid camps were Yudhishthira's work.

questions.q: When Shalya offered a boon to his unknown host, what did Duryodhana ask?
options: The Madra cavalry as a gift
A vow never to fight Karna
That Shalya fight on the Kaurava side
questions.explanation: Duryodhana revealed himself and asked Shalya to lead his army for the Kauravas, and the king felt bound by his word.

questions.q: Why did Karna want Shalya, of all men, as his charioteer?
options: Shalya's skill with horses matched Krishna's
Shalya's presence shielded him from the twins
No other king would serve him
questions.explanation: To equal Arjuna, who had Krishna at the reins, Karna needed the one driver whose horsemanship rivaled Krishna's own.

questions.q: On what condition did Shalya agree to drive Karna's chariot?
options: That he command the army afterward
That he might say whatever he pleased
That Karna never question his driving
questions.explanation: Insulted at serving a charioteer's son, the king consented only on the condition that he could speak as he wished before Karna.

questions.q: Which fable did Shalya tell to deflate Karna's boasting before the duel with Arjuna?
options: The crow who challenged the swans
The frog in the dried-up well
questions.explanation: Shalya told of a pampered crow that challenged swans over the ocean and drowned, comparing Karna's challenge to Arjuna.

questions.q: Who proposed Shalya as commander after Karna's death?
questions.explanation: On the morning after Karna fell, Ashwatthama suggested that the mighty Shalya lead the remaining Kaurava host.

questions.q: How long did Shalya lead the Kaurava army as its commander?
options: From dawn to dusk of one day
questions.explanation: Shalya commanded only from the eighteenth morning until midday, when Yudhishthira slew him.

questions.q: With what weapon did Yudhishthira finally slay Shalya?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira hurled a blazing shakti, a dart-like spear, that pierced Shalya's chest and ended the duel.

questions.q: Why did Yudhishthira, mildest of the brothers, face Shalya in the final duel?
options: He owed Shalya a debt of honor
Krishna had allotted Shalya as Yudhishthira's share
No other Pandava dared to fight him
Shalya had insulted him at the dice game
questions.explanation: Krishna designated Shalya as Yudhishthira's portion of the war, telling the king of dharma that this kill belonged to him.

questions.q: Which young prince did Shalya kill on the first day of the war?
questions.explanation: On the opening day Shalya slew Prince Uttara of Matsya, the same youth who had once ridden with Brihannala.

questions.q: In an earlier brawl at Draupadi's swayamvara, which Pandava threw Shalya down?
questions.explanation: When the angry kings attacked after the contest, Bhima grappled with Shalya and hurled him to the ground.

questions.q: Whom did Shalya keep praising from the chariot box to sap Karna's spirit?
questions.explanation: Shalya repeatedly extolled Arjuna's prowess and predicted Karna's defeat, fulfilling his secret pledge to demoralize the Kaurava champion.

questions.q: What was the capital city of Shalya's kingdom of Madra?
questions.explanation: Madra's capital was Sakala, identified with the modern city of Sialkot in the Punjab region.

questions.q: By which patronymic is Shalya addressed in the epic?
questions.explanation: Shalya bears the patronymic Artayani, marking his descent in the royal line of Madra.

questions.q: How large was the force Shalya brought to the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: Shalya marched with one full akshauhini, which ended up swelling the Kaurava count of eleven.

questions.q: In the succession of Kaurava commanders, where did Shalya stand?
options: Fourth, after Bhishma, Drona, and Karna
questions.explanation: Shalya was the fourth and last commander-in-chief, following Bhishma, Drona, and Karna in turn.

questions.q: What did Karna attack in his furious replies to Shalya's taunts?
options: The morals and customs of the Madra people
questions.explanation: Karna retaliated with scathing tales about the conduct and customs of the Madrakas, trading insult for insult as they rode.

questions.q: How does the epic picture the slain Shalya lying upon the field?
options: Like a sun fallen at midday
Like a felled banner of Indra
Like a tree struck by lightning
Like a beloved wife embracing her lord
questions.explanation: The Shalya Parva describes the fallen king embracing the earth like a dear wife embracing her beloved lord.

questions.q: What rite marked Shalya's appointment as commander on the final morning?
options: He swore an oath upon Karna's bow
Duryodhana consecrated him with sanctified water
Bhishma blessed him from the arrow bed
The army circled him thrice in silence
questions.explanation: Duryodhana formally anointed Shalya as commander with vessels of sanctified water before the last day's battle.

questions.q: Where does the Shalya Parva stand among the epic's eighteen books?
questions.explanation: The Shalya Parva is the ninth of the Mahabharata's eighteen books, covering the final day of the war.
